title: Data Analysis Without Code
summary: Use AI to explore, explain, and communicate data when the job is analytical thinking, pattern finding, and decision support.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: What AI Can And Cannot Do With Data
lessons.subtitle: AI can help interpret and communicate data, but the workflow needs care.
lessons.objective: Use AI for data-adjacent analysis while understanding where verification and tooling still matter.
lessons.useWhen: AI can help interpret and communicate data, but the workflow needs care.
lessons.whyItMatters: People often ask AI for statistical certainty or hidden truth when what they really need is structured exploration and explanation.

coreConcepts.title: Good At Framing And Explaining
coreConcepts.text: AI is useful for asking questions, spotting patterns, and drafting explanations from structured inputs.

coreConcepts.title: Weak Without Reliable Inputs
coreConcepts.text: Messy data descriptions or missing context produce shallow insights and invented interpretations.

coreConcepts.title: Verification Remains Essential
coreConcepts.text: Numbers, formulas, and conclusions still need checking against the real dataset or source system.

workedExample.scenario: An analyst wants help interpreting weekly performance data.
workedExample.approach: They describe the metrics, time period, and business context before asking for patterns and questions to investigate.
workedExample.result: The AI output becomes a useful thinking aid rather than fake certainty.
workedExample.verification: The analyst checks any claimed trend or number against the actual dashboard.

doThisNow.task: Write a brief for one dataset or dashboard you need to interpret this week.
steps: List what must be checked manually.

commonMistakes: Asking for insights without defining the metrics.
Trusting numbers from the model without checking.
Expecting AI to replace analysis discipline.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to compare options, pressure-test plans, and prepare clearer stakeholder communication.
roleVariants.writer: Turn rough thinking into structured documents, memos, and decision briefs.
roleVariants.analyst: Ask for assumptions, ranges, and missing data before acting on recommendations.
roleVariants.operator: Translate ambiguous work into checklists, workflows, and operating decisions.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Describe The Data Well
lessons.subtitle: The model needs metric definitions, context, and comparison points.
lessons.objective: Provide data context so AI can help with interpretation rather than guessing.
lessons.useWhen: The model needs metric definitions, context, and comparison points.
lessons.whyItMatters: Poor data descriptions lead to generic commentary that sounds analytical without being useful.

coreConcepts.title: Define The Measures
coreConcepts.text: State what each metric means and why it matters.

coreConcepts.title: Add Baselines
coreConcepts.text: Comparisons to prior periods, targets, or segments make interpretation more grounded.

coreConcepts.title: Explain The Situation
coreConcepts.text: Recent events, campaign changes, staffing shifts, or product launches often explain the numbers better than the numbers alone.

workedExample.scenario: A manager pastes weekly numbers with no labels and gets vague commentary.
workedExample.approach: They rerun the task with metric definitions, target ranges, and context on a recent launch.
workedExample.result: The analysis becomes more relevant and less generic.
workedExample.verification: They confirm that any trend explanation is supported by known events or clearly marked as a hypothesis.

doThisNow.task: Rewrite one data prompt to include definitions, baselines, and context.
steps: Mark what is known vs hypothesized.

roleVariants.manager: Use AI to compare options, pressure-test plans, and prepare clearer stakeholder communication.
roleVariants.writer: Turn rough thinking into structured documents, memos, and decision briefs.
roleVariants.analyst: Ask for assumptions, ranges, and missing data before acting on recommendations.
roleVariants.operator: Translate ambiguous work into checklists, workflows, and operating decisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Generate Questions And Hypotheses
lessons.subtitle: One of AI’s best uses is widening the set of reasonable questions.
lessons.objective: Use AI to generate follow-up questions, hypotheses, and segments worth investigating.
lessons.useWhen: One of AI’s best uses is widening the set of reasonable questions.
lessons.whyItMatters: Analysis improves when you ask better questions, not only when you draft cleaner charts.

coreConcepts.title: Ask For Possibilities, Not Certainty
coreConcepts.text: Prompt for plausible explanations and next checks rather than definitive claims.

coreConcepts.title: Prioritize By Decision Value
coreConcepts.text: The best follow-up questions are the ones that could change an action or decision.

coreConcepts.title: Use AI To Surface Blind Spots
coreConcepts.text: The model can suggest segments, time windows, or comparison points you did not initially consider.

workedExample.scenario: A dashboard shows declining conversion but no obvious root cause.
workedExample.approach: The analyst asks AI for plausible hypotheses, segmented cuts to inspect, and what evidence would confirm each one.
workedExample.result: The next analysis step becomes clearer.
workedExample.verification: Each hypothesis is tested against actual data rather than accepted as the explanation.

artifact.label: Prompt Pattern
artifact.title: Hypothesis request
items: What data would test each explanation

doThisNow.task: Use AI to generate three testable hypotheses from one business metric change.
steps: Select the one most worth checking.

commonMistakes: Asking AI to name the root cause directly.
Not defining how a hypothesis would be tested.
Using too many low-value follow-up questions.

roleVariants.writer: Turn rough thinking into structured documents, memos, and decision briefs.
roleVariants.analyst: Ask for assumptions, ranges, and missing data before acting on recommendations.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Turn Analysis Into Communication
lessons.subtitle: Insights only matter when other people can understand and act on them.
lessons.objective: Draft clear narratives, readouts, and chart explanations from analytical work.
lessons.useWhen: Insights only matter when other people can understand and act on them.
lessons.whyItMatters: Many data problems are communication problems. AI can help translate analysis into decision-friendly language.

coreConcepts.title: Separate Insight From Evidence
coreConcepts.text: State the observation, then the likely implication, then the evidence that supports it.

coreConcepts.title: Write For The Reader
coreConcepts.text: Leaders want implications. Working teams often need the operational detail behind the conclusion.

coreConcepts.title: Keep Confidence Honest
coreConcepts.text: Be explicit about what is observed, what is inferred, and what still needs analysis.

workedExample.scenario: An analyst has solid findings but struggles to present them to non-technical stakeholders.
workedExample.approach: They ask AI to draft a short readout using observed trend, likely implication, and suggested action.
workedExample.result: The story becomes easier to grasp.
workedExample.verification: The analyst checks that the final narrative does not claim more certainty than the data supports.

doThisNow.task: Turn one chart or dashboard finding into a short executive readout.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Build A Data Interpretation Toolkit
lessons.subtitle: Save the prompts and formats that support recurring analysis work.
lessons.objective: Create reusable assets for framing, hypothesis generation, and readout drafting.
lessons.useWhen: Save the prompts and formats that support recurring analysis work.
lessons.whyItMatters: Analytical quality improves when you reuse proven questions and communication structures.

coreConcepts.title: Store The Data Brief
coreConcepts.text: Keep the input pattern that produced a good analysis session.

coreConcepts.title: Store The Hypothesis Prompt
coreConcepts.text: Save the prompts that lead to useful next questions.

coreConcepts.title: Store The Readout Format
coreConcepts.text: A strong narrative structure is as reusable as a strong analysis prompt.

workedExample.scenario: A business analyst repeatedly explains weekly KPIs to different audiences.
workedExample.approach: They build a toolkit with a data brief, a hypothesis prompt, and two readout formats.
workedExample.result: The weekly cycle becomes faster and more consistent.
workedExample.verification: They refresh the toolkit when changes in the dashboard make an old prompt misleading.

doThisNow.task: Save one analysis workflow as a reusable toolkit entry.

commonMistakes: Letting the toolkit drift when metrics change.

nextModuleBridge: Next, use AI to support higher-level choices, tradeoffs, and strategic reasoning.
